mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates directly increase the discount rate for equity valuations, mechanically depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical decline following rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp, or the signal loses predictive power during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand strength that lifts corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both increase MoM
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 60-day forward returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects constrained output capacity that forces firms to cut capital expenditures, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles produce positive 20-day returns in > 60% of observations, or the mechanism fails to outperform a buy-and-hold baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL reading above 310.0 indicates entrenched inflation that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day returns in > 70% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 310.0, or the threshold loses predictive accuracy in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a stable unemployment rate signals robust job creation without wage inflation pressure, supporting consumer confidence and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered months, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly increases corporate revenue baselines, mechanically lifting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month through improved earnings forecasts.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows a negative or zero correlation with 20-day forward returns over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates rapidly expanding manufacturing capacity that boosts equity risk premiums, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration during recovery phases produces negative 5-day returns in > 65% of cases, or the signal fails to maintain positive alpha after transaction costs.
